summary: Reverse shell and host recon exfiltration to hardcoded C2 on require
details: index.js executes on require as an IIFE that opens a bash reverse shell via /dev/tcp to the hardcoded IP 103.27.109.184 on port 8895, granting interactive remote shell control of the installer's host. In parallel, it collects host reconnaissance (hostname, host id, IP addresses, docker state, and full environment variables via execSync of `env`) and transmits the JSON to 103.27.109.184:8890. When docker is present, it launches an alpine container mounting the host filesystem at /host to read /etc/shadow and probes a local admin endpoint with default credentials, sending results to 103.27.109.184:8891. The package name imitates an n8n community-node helper but the shipped code is a pure remote-access implant with no legitimate functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
